note: Marginalized position CRLB (position block of the full 6x6 F^-1) vs naive (inverse of the F_pp block alone, ignoring orientation). The ratio is the coupling penalty from estimating unknown orientation (Schur complement) and is a pose-INVARIANT constant ~2.952 (variance ~8.7x) for the co-located triad/triad geometry: the honest 6-DOF position CRLB is ~3x worse than an orientation-known bound. Orientation CRLB scales as z^3.0 (one power less than position's z^4, because orientation reads the field itself while position reads its gradient).
